summary_strategy: Anchor summary in operational reliability and cross-functional tool-building, emphasizing deployable controls, incident response automation, and cloud-native security services — mirroring Apex Systems’ pragmatic, action-oriented tone.

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, PowerShell, Bash, Java, and Go — the top 5 languages most relevant to security automation and cloud tooling per JD requirements; keep all 11 items unchanged.
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to lead with CrowdStrike Falcon/RTR, Rapid7 InsightVM, SIEM, SOAR, and XDR; add 'Defender XDR' and 'Carbon Black' from Skills Inventory to strengthen endpoint security alignment.
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Reorder to highlight RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, NLP, and retrieval tuning first; add 'Embeddings-based retrieval' and 'AI governance and auditability' from Skills Inventory to emphasize operational AI for SecOps.
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI, Docker, and React.js — aligning with API-driven security tooling and internal service development; keep all 9 items unchanged.
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with AWS ECS, IAM, Secrets management, CI/CD pipelines, and Infrastructure as Code; add 'Terraform', 'Ansible', 'GitHub Actions' from Skills Inventory to strengthen cloud automation credibility.
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, vector databases, Snowflake, ClickHouse, and Redis — reflecting data correlation, analytics, and RAG pipeline needs; keep all 10 items unchanged.

experience_focus.company: University of California, Office of the President
experience_focus.must_highlight: Coraline’s multi-source ingestion and hierarchical confidence-matching for asset reconciliation; API-driven vulnerability response automation; secure server infrastructure with IAM/MFA integration
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No invented metrics (e.g., avoid 'reduced MTTR by X%'), no unsupported leadership claims like 'led team of N engineers'

experience_focus.must_highlight: Python/PowerShell automation for fleet-wide patching and BitLocker enforcement; Microsoft Graph API distribution list tooling; phishing analysis pipelines with KnowBe4
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices; 200 hours saved annually; 500 hours reduced workload; 25 percent compliance improvement
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No vague claims like 'improved security posture' without quantifiable tie-in; no assumption of OSCP/GPEN-level testing depth

experience_focus.must_highlight: CI/CD pipelines with automated build/deploy; Selenium-based accessibility and cross-browser testing
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No inflated client count or revenue impact; no assumption of production scale beyond stated freelance scope

risk_controls: Never invent metrics — only reuse numbers explicitly present in baseline resume or analysis mapping
Do not add certifications beyond AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner (January 2025)
Avoid phrases like 'seasoned', 'veteran', or 'thought leader'; use 'built', 'engineered', 'delivered' instead
Do not claim penetration testing experience beyond what is stated (i.e., no OSCP/GPEN mention unless certified)
Do not merge or rephrase evidence quotes — keep direct phrasing from analysis mapping where relevant
